content: agentfront issue #35 spec CONVERGED (docs/specs/2026-06-26-agentfront-ships-a-public-consumer-cli-api-a-host.md) via /think. Key DECISION (user, 2026-06-26): the MCP surface ships as a SINGLE-DISPATCH "CLI on MCP" tool ONLY — one MCP tool taking a command-path + args, dispatching through the App registry, structured {code,message,remediation} on failure — NOT N tools. This CONFLICTS with colleague’s converged spec (colleague repo docs/specs/2026-06-25-colleague-s-agent-first-cli-is-rendered-from-an-im.md), whose honesty condition asserts CLI/MCP/learn enumerate the SAME set (= N MCP tools). RESOLUTION: colleague MUST revise that set-equality condition to compare the single tool’s command catalog (colleague-side follow-up; coordinate via an agentculture/colleague issue). Other agentfront #35 decisions: grow App.cli() to dispatch registered tools (not a new build_cli); nested groups via explicit group= on registration; app.add_command(name,handler) for host-owned launcher verbs (session/tui); promote AfiError {code,message,remediation} to a PUBLIC importable module (e.g. agentfront.errors, no argparse import) so consumers stop reaching into agentfront.cli._errors; reuse the internal agentfront/cli + explain + overview machinery by parameterizing prog/version/issues_url out of _brand; pure-stdlib (mcp stays the [mcp] extra). explain/overview are NOT registry-derived today (hand-authored catalog + filesystem walk) — the consumer API must add a registry-derivation path.
